OpenTelemetry tracing layer setup (plan §10).\n//!\n//! When `tracing.enabled: false`, this module is a no-op — zero overhead.\n//! When enabled, it initializes an OTLP exporter with head-based sampling.\n\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n\n#[cfg(feature = \"tracing\")]\nuse opentelemetry::trace::TracerProvider;\n\n/// Initialize the OpenTelemetry tracing layer if enabled in config.\n///\n/// Returns `Some(layer)` when tracing is enabled, `None` otherwise.\n/// The caller is responsible for adding the layer to the subscriber.\n#[cfg(feature = \"tracing\")]\npub fn init_otel_layer(config: &MiroirConfig) -> Option<tracing_opentelemetry::OpenTelemetryLayer<tracing_subscriber::Registry, opentelemetry_sdk::trace::Tracer>> {\n    if !config.tracing.enabled {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    use opentelemetry_sdk::{\n        trace::{TracerProvider as SdkTracerProvider, Sampler, Tracer},\n        Resource,\n    };\n    use opentelemetry_sdk::propagation::TraceContextPropagator;\n    use tracing_opentelemetry::OpenTelemetryLayer;\n    use opentelemetry_otlp::WithExportConfig;\n    use opentelemetry::KeyValue;\n\n    // Set global propagator for distributed tracing\n    opentelemetry::global::set_text_map_propagator(TraceContextPropagator::new());\n\n    // Build resource attributes (service.name, service.version, host.name)\n    let pod_name = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n    let resource = Resource::new(vec![\n        KeyValue::new(\"service.name\", config.tracing.service_name.clone()),\n        KeyValue::new(\"service.version\", env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\")),\n        KeyValue::new(\"host.name\", pod_name),\n    ]);\n\n    // Create OTLP exporter with tonic (gRPC) transport\n    let exporter = opentelemetry_otlp::SpanExporter::builder()\n        .with_tonic()\n        .with_endpoint(&config.tracing.endpoint)\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            eprintln!(\"Failed to create OTLP exporter: {}\", e);\n            e\n        })\n        .ok()?;\n\n    // Head-based sampler: sample_rate fraction of traces\n    let sampler = Sampler::TraceIdRatioBased(config.tracing.sample_rate);\n\n    // Build provider with exporter, sampler, and resource\n    let provider = SdkTracerProvider::builder()\n        .with_simple_exporter(exporter)\n        .with_sampler(sampler)\n        .with_resource(resource)\n        .build();\n\n    // Get the tracer from the provider\n    let tracer = provider.tracer(\"miroir-proxy\");\n\n    // Set global tracer provider\n    let _ = opentelemetry::global::set_tracer_provider(provider);\n\n    // Create and return the tracing layer\n    let layer = tracing_opentelemetry::layer().with_tracer(tracer);\n\n    Some(layer)\n}\n\n/// Shutdown the OpenTelemetry tracer provider, flushing any pending spans.\n///\n/// This should be called during graceful shutdown to ensure all in-flight\n/// traces are exported before the process exits.\n#[cfg(feature = \"tracing\")]\npub fn shutdown_otel() {\n    use opentelemetry::global;\n    // Flush any remaining traces\n    let _ = global::shutdown_tracer_provider();\n}\n\n/// No-op implementation when tracing feature is disabled.\n#[cfg(not(feature = \"tracing\"))]\npub fn init_otel_layer(_config: &MiroirConfig) -> Option<tracing_subscriber::layer::Identity> {\n    None\n}\n\n/// No-op shutdown when tracing feature is disabled.\n#[cfg(not(feature = \"tracing\"))]\npub fn shutdown_otel() {\n anyhow::Result<()> {\n148\t    // Load configuration (file → env → CLI overlay)\n149\t    let config = MiroirConfig::load()\n150\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Failed to load config: {}\", .with_span_list(false);\n170\t        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n171\t        registry()\n172\t            .with(otel_layer)\n173\t            .with(filter)\n174\t            .with(json_layer)\n175\t            .init();\n176\t    } else {\n177\t        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n178\t            .json()\n179\t .with(filter)\n185\t            .with(json_layer)\n186\t            .init();\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n190\t    // This is });\n236\t\n237\t        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n238\t        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n239\t        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n240\t        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n241\t        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n242\t        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n243\t        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n244\t        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n245\t        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n246\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n247\t            info!(\"starting admin session revocation subscriber\");\n248\t            if let Err(e) = miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n249\t                &redis_url,\n250\t                &key_prefix,\n251\t                move |session_id: String| {\n252\t                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n253\t                    revoked_total.inc();\n254\t                },\n255\t            )\n256\t            .await\n257\t            {\n258\t                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n259\t            }\n260\t        });\n261\t    }\n262\t\n263\t    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n264\t    let app = Router::new()\n265\t        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n266\t        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n267\t        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n268\t        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n269\t        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n270\t        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n271\t        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n272\t        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n273\t        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n274\t        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n275\t        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n276\t        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n277\t        // 2. auth_middleware\n278\t        // 3. Extension layers\n279\t        // 4. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n280\t        // 5. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n281\t        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n282\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n283\t            auth::CsrfState {\n284\t                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n285\t                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n286\t            },\n287\t            auth::csrf_middleware,\n288\t        ))\n289\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n290\t            state.auth.clone(),\n291\t            auth::auth_middleware,\n292\t        ))\n293\t        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n294\t        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n295\t        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n296\t            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n297\t        ))\n298\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n299\t            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n300\t        ))\n301\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n302\t            TelemetryState {\n303\t                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n304\t                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n305\t            },\n306\t            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n307\t        ))\n308\t        .with_state(state.clone());\n309\t\n310\t    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind,  let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n323\t    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n324\t\n325\t    // Spawn main server with shutdown\n330\t    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n331\t    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n332\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n333\t\n334\t    // Run both servers concurrently\n335\t    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n336\t        main_server,\n337\t        metrics_server\n338\t    );\n339\t\n340\t    // Check for errors\n341\t    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n342\t        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n343\t    }\n344\t    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n345\t        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n346\t    }\n347\t\n348\t    Ok(())\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n352\t///\n353\t/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n354\t/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\n355\tasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n356\t    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n357\t        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n358\t    ));\n359\t\n360\t    loop {\n361\t        interval.tick().await;\n362\t\n363\t        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n364\t        let mut all_healthy = true;\n365\t\n366\t        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n367\t        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n368\t\n369\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n370\t            // Get current node status\n371\t            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n372\t\n373\t            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n374\t            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n375\t                continue;\n376\t            }\n377\t\n378\t            // Get node address\n379\t            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n380\t                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n381\t                None => {\n382\t                    all_healthy = false;\n383\t                    continue;\n384\t                }\n385\t            };\n386\t\n387\t            // Try to reach the node\n388\t            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n389\t                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n390\t                .build()\n391\t            {\n392\t                Ok(c) => c,\n393\t                Err(_) => {\n394\t                    all_healthy = false;\n395\t                    continue;\n396\t                }\n397\t            };\n398\t\n399\t            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n400\t            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n401\t\n402\t            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n403\t                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n404\t                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n405\t                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n406\t                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n407\t                }\n408\t            } else {\n409\t                all_healthy = false;\n410\t            }\n411\t        }\n412\t\n413\t        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n414\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n415\t            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n416\t            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n417\t        }\n418\t\n419\t        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n420\t        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n421\t\n422\t        // Update task registry size gauge\n423\t        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n424\t        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n425\t\n426\t        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n427\t        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n428\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n429\t        }\n430\t\n431\t        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n432\t        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n433\t    }\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n437\t/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\n438\tfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n439\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n440\t    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n441\t    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n442\t\n443\t    // Per-node shard count\n444\t    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n445\t        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n446\t\n447\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n448\t        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n449\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n450\t            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n451\t                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n452\t            );\n453\t            for node_id in &assigned {\n454\t                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n460\t                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n461\t                }\n462\t            }\n463\t        }\n464\t topo.shards > 0 {\n472\t        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n473\t    } else {\n474\t        1.0\n475\t    };\n476\t    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n477\t    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n478\t\n479\t    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n480\t        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n481\t    }\n482\t}\n483\t\n484\t/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n485\t///\n486\t/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n488\t/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\n489\tfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n490\t    // ── Memory pressure ──\n491\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n492\t    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n493\t    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n494\t\n495\t    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n496\t        if max > 0 {\n497\t            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n498\t            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n499\t            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n500\t        }\n501\t    }\n502\t\n503\t    // ── CPU throttling ──\n504\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n505\t    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n506\t        for line in contents.lines() {\n507\t            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n508\t                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n509\t                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n510\t                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n511\t                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n512\t                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n513\t                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n514\t                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n515\t                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n516\t                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n517\t                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n518\t                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n519\t                }\n520\t            }\n521\t        }\n522\t    }\n523\t\n524\t    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n525\t    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n526\t    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n527\t    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n528\t    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n529\t    metrics.set_leader(true);\n530\t    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\n534\tfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n535\t    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n536\t        .ok()\n537\t        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n538\t}\n539\t\n540\t/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\n541\tasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n542\t    let ctrl_c = async {\n543\t        signal::ctrl_c()\n544\t            .await\n545\t            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n546\t    };\n547\t\n548\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n549\t    let terminate = async {\n550\t        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n551\t            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n552\t            .recv()\n553\t            .await;\n554\t    };\n555\t\n556\t    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n557\t    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n558\t\n559\t    tokio::select! {\n560\t        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n561\t        _ = terminate => {},\n562\t    }\n563\t\n564\t    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n565\t\n566\t    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n567\t logging (plan §10 format)\n    // Fields on every line: timestamp, level, target, message, pod_id\n    // Per-request fields (request_id) are added by telemetry middleware span.\n    let filter = Some(otel_layer) = otel::init_otel_layer(&config) {\n        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n            .json()\n            .flatten_event(true)\n            .with_target(true)\n            .with_current_span(true)\n            .with_span_list(false);\n        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n        registry()\n            .with(otel_layer)\n            .with(filter)\n            .with(json_layer)\n            .init();\n    } else {\n        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n            .json()\n            .flatten_event(true)\n            .with_target(true)\n            .with_current_span(true)\n            .with_span_list(false);\n        registry()\n            .with(filter)\n            .with(json_layer)\n            .init();\n    }\n\n    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n    // This is done via a separate info span that is entered once and config.shards,\n        replication_factor = config.replication_factor,\n        replica_groups = config.replica_groups,\n        \"miroir-proxy starting\"\n    );\n\n    // Validate critical secrets at startup (plan §9: \"orchestrator refuses to\n    // start the search UI without it\").\n    if config.search_ui.enabled {\n        let jwt_env = &config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_env;\n        match std::env::var(jwt_env) {\n            Ok(v) if !v.is_empty() => {}\n            _ => {\n                anyhow::bail!(\n                    \"search_ui is enabled but {} is not set — refusing to tokio::spawn(async move {\n            scoped_key_rotation::run_scoped_key_rotator(rotation_state).await;\n        });\n\n        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            info!(\"starting admin session revocation subscriber\");\n            if let Err(e) = miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n                &redis_url,\n                &key_prefix,\n                move |session_id: String| {\n                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n                    revoked_total.inc();\n                },\n            )\n            .await\n            {\n                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n        // 2. auth_middleware\n        // 3. Extension layers\n        // 4. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n        // 5. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            auth::CsrfState {\n                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n            },\n            auth::csrf_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state.auth.clone(),\n            auth::auth_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            TelemetryState {\n                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n            },\n            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .with_state(state.clone());\n\n    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n\n    info!(\n        main_addr = %main_addr,\n        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n        \"listening\"\n    );\n\n    // Create listeners\n    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n\n    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Run both servers concurrently\n    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n        main_server,\n        metrics_server\n    );\n\n    // Check for errors\n    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n///\n/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\nasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n    ));\n\n    loop {\n        interval.tick().await;\n\n        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n        let mut all_healthy = true;\n\n        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            // Get current node status\n            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n\n            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Get node address\n            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n                None => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Try to reach the node\n            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n                .build()\n            {\n                Ok(c) => c,\n                Err(_) => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n\n            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n                }\n            } else {\n                all_healthy = false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n        }\n\n        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n\n        // Update task registry size gauge\n        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n\n        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n            state.mark_ready().await;\n        }\n\n        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\nfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n\n    // Per-node shard count\n    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n            );\n            for node_id in &assigned {\n                let healthy = node_map\n                    .get(node_id)\n                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n                if healthy {\n                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        if has_healthy_replica {\n            healthy_shards += 1;\n        } else {\n            degraded_shards += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n    } else {\n        1.0\n    };\n    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n\n    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n///\n/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\nfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    // ── Memory pressure ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n\n    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n        if max > 0 {\n            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── CPU throttling ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n        for line in contents.lines() {\n            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n    metrics.set_leader(true);\n    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n}\n\n/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\nfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n        .ok()\n        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n}\n\n/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\nasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n    let ctrl_c = async {\n        signal::ctrl_c()\n            .await\n            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    let terminate = async {\n        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n            .recv()\n            .await;\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n\n    tokio::select! {\n        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n        _ = terminate => {},\n    }\n\n    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n\n    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n    otel::shutdown_otel();\n}\n","numLines":569,"startLine":1,"totalLines":569}}}
