P7.5 Structured JSON logging + request IDs + trace correlation tests.\n//!\n//! Tests for plan §10 structured logging format:\n//! - JSON-per-line parseable by jq\n//! - request_id on every log line (same as X-Request-Id header)\n//! - No PII (API keys, document content, query strings) in logs\n//! - Log volume: 2 INFO entries per search request (middleware + search handler)\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse axum::{\n    extract::Request,\n    body::Body,\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Router,\n};\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\n\n/// Helper: parse a JSON log line and extract fields\nfn parse_log_line(line: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {\n    serde_json::from_str(line).ok()\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a string is a valid 8-char hex request ID (from RequestId::new)\nfn is_valid_request_id(s: &str) -> bool {\n    s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit())\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a line contains PII patterns\nfn contains_pii(line: &str) -> bool {\n    let lower = line.to_lowercase();\n    // Check for API key patterns\n    if lower.contains(\"api_key\") || lower.contains(\"apikey\") || lower.contains(\"api-key\") {\n        // Allow if it's just \"api_key_hash\" or similar\n        if !lower.contains(\"hash\") && !lower.contains(\"uid\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    // Check for master keys\n    if lower.contains(\"master\") && (lower.contains(\"key\") || lower.contains(\"secret\")) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for JWT tokens\n    if lower.contains(\"eyj\") && lower.len() > 100 {\n        // JWT base64 pattern\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for document content patterns (long text blocks in logs)\n    if line.len() > 5000 {\n        // Very long log line might contain document content\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for raw query strings (both URL-style q= and JSON-style \"q\":)\n    if (lower.contains(\"q=\") || lower.contains(\"\\\"q\\\":\")) && line.len() > 200 {\n        // But not if the value is explicitly redacted\n        if !lower.contains(\"\\\"[redacted]\\\"\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    false\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 1: jq parses every log line\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_json_logs_parseable_by_jq() {\n    // Sample log entry in the expected format\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"GET /indexes/products/search 200\",\n        \"request_id\": \"abc123\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"miroir-proxy-7f8d9c6d5-abc12\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42,\n        \"status\": 200,\n        \"method\": \"GET\",\n        \"path_template\": \"/indexes/{uid}/search\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log);\n    assert!(parsed.is_some(), \"Sample log should parse as JSON\");\n\n    let log_obj = parsed.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"level\"], \"info\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"target\"], \"miroir.request\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"request_id\"], \"abc123\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"duration_ms\"], 42);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_format_in_logs() {\n    // Verify request_id appears in JSON logs\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"search completed\",\n        \"request_id\": \"a1b2c3d4\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"test-pod\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log).unwrap();\n    let request_id = parsed[\"request_id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n\n    // Request IDs should be 8 hex chars (from RequestId::new())\n    assert_eq!(request_id.len(), 8);\n    assert!(request_id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 2: request_id traceable across pods\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_extraction_from_logs() {\n    let logs = vec![\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"message\":\"GET /search 200\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.001Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"node_id\":\"node-1\",\"message\":\"node call started\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.010Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"index\":\"products\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    // Extract all logs with request_id = \"abc12345\"\n    let target_id = \"abc12345\";\n    let matching_logs: Vec<_> = logs\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(&format!(\"\\\"request_id\\\":\\\"{}\\\"\", target_id)))\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(matching_logs.len(), 3, \"Should find all 3 log lines for the request\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 3: No PII in logs\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_api_keys_in_logs() {\n    // These patterns should NOT appear in logs\n    let pii_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key\": \"master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"bearer\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"authorization\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in pii_patterns {\n        assert!(\n            contains_pii(pattern),\n            \"Pattern should be flagged as PII: {}\",\n            pattern\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Hashed versions are OK\n    let safe_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key_hash\": \"a1b2c3d4\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"key_uid\": \"12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"tenant_id\": \"tenant-123\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in safe_patterns {\n        assert!(\n            !contains_pii(pattern),\n            \"Hashed pattern should NOT be flagged as PII: {}\",\n            pattern\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_query_strings_in_logs() {\n    // Raw Meilisearch query content should not be logged\n    // The contains_pii function checks for long q= patterns (user queries)\n    // Line must be >200 chars total and contain \"q=\" to trigger PII detection\n    let padding = \"x\".repeat(200);\n    let bad_log = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = 'test@example.com'\", \"padding\": \"{}\"}}\"#, padding);\n    assert!(contains_pii(&bad_log), \"Long query string should be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Redacted queries are OK\n    let good_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"query\": \"[redacted]\", \"q\": \"[redacted]\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(good_log), \"Redacted query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Short queries are OK (e.g., just a word or two)\n    let short_query = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"test\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(short_query), \"Short query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Long line without q= is OK\n    let long_safe = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing\", \"data\": \"{}\"}}\"#, \"x\".repeat(200));\n    assert!(!contains_pii(&long_safe), \"Long line without q= should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_document_content_in_logs() {\n    // Large JSON blocks likely contain document content\n    let large_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"indexing document\", \"doc\": {\"#;\n    let large_log_padded = format!(\"{}{}\", large_log, \"x\".repeat(5000));\n    assert!(contains_pii(&large_log_padded), \"Very long log line should be flagged as potential PII\");\n\n    // Normal log lines are OK\n    let normal_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"indexed 100 documents\", \"count\": 100}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(normal_log), \"Normal log line should NOT be flagged\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 4: Log volume\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_log_volume_info_level() {\n    // At INFO level, search requests produce 2 INFO log entries:\n    // 1 from telemetry middleware (miroir.request) + 1 from search handler (miroir.search)\n    let request_logs = vec![\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"GET /indexes/products/search 200\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.001Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"message\":\"node call\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.002Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    let info_count = request_logs\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(r#\"\"level\":\"info\"\"#))\n        .count();\n\n    assert_eq!(\n        info_count, 2,\n        \"INFO level should have 2 log lines per search request (middleware + search handler)\"\n    );\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_debug_level_has_more_logs() {\n    // At DEBUG level, we get per-node logs in addition to the INFO logs\n    let debug_logs = vec![\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"GET / 200\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.001Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"message\":\"node call started\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.002Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"message\":\"node call completed\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.003Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    let debug_count = debug_logs\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(r#\"\"level\":\"debug\"\"#))\n        .count();\n\n    assert!(debug_count > 1, \"DEBUG level should have multiple log lines\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Integration test: Verify actual log format from middleware\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_middleware_log_format() {\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{generate_request_id, RequestIdExt};\n    use axum::http::HeaderMap;\n\n    // Test request_id generation format\n    let id = generate_request_id();\n    assert_eq!(id.len(), 16, \"Request ID should be 16 hex chars\");\n    assert!(id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()), \"Request ID should be hexadecimal\");\n\n    // Test header extraction\n    let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n    headers.set_request_id(\"test123456789abcd\");\n    assert_eq!(headers.get_request_id(), Some(\"test123456789abcd\".to_string()));\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Log level verification\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_log_levels_correct() {\n    let logs_by_level = vec![\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"error\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"internal failure\"}\"#, \"error\"),\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"warn\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"degraded response\"}\"#, \"warn\"),\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"GET / 200\"}\"#, \"info\"),\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"message\":\"node call\"}\"#, \"debug\"),\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"trace\",\"target\":\"miroir.scatter\",\"message\":\"fan-out buffer\"}\"#, \"trace\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (log, expected_level) in logs_by_level {\n        let parsed = parse_log_line(log).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(parsed[\"level\"], expected_level);\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Verify SearchRequestBody Debug impl redacts sensitive fields\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n    // Verified by unit test in routes/search.rs (SearchRequestBody is private).\n    // That test confirms the Debug impl redacts `q` and `filter` fields.\n    // This placeholder keeps the integration test file's acceptance criteria visible.\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Request ID middleware integration tests (P7.5.a)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_response_header() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Every response includes X-Request-Id: <8-char hex>\n    use axum::routing::get;\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::request_id_middleware;\n\n    async fn handler() -> &'static str {\n        \"ok\"\n    }\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(handler))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    let headers = response.headers();\n    let request_id = headers.get(\"x-request-id\");\n    assert!(request_id.is_some(), \"X-Request-Id header should be present\");\n\n    let id_str = request_id.unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(id_str),\n        \"X-Request-Id should be 8 hex chars, got: {}\",\n        id_str\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_extension_accessible() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>() works from any handler\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n            assert!(\n                is_valid_request_id(id.as_str()),\n                \"RequestId in extensions should be 8 hex chars\"\n            );\n            \"ok\"\n        }))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_consecutive_requests_different_ids() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Two consecutive requests produce different IDs\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::extract::State as AxumState;\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n    use std::sync::Mutex;\n\n    let captured_ids = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::<String>::new()));\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>, AxumState(ids): AxumState<Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>>| async move {\n                ids.lock().unwrap().push(id.as_str().to_string());\n                \"ok\"\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware))\n        .with_state(captured_ids.clone());\n\n    // Make two requests consecutively\n    for _ in 0..2 {\n        let _ = app\n            .clone()\n            .oneshot(\n                Request::builder()\n                    .uri(\"/test\")\n                    .body(Body::empty())\n                    .unwrap(),\n            )\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    let ids = captured_ids.lock().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(ids.len(), 2, \"Should have captured 2 request IDs\");\n    assert_ne!(ids[0], ids[1], \"Two consecutive requests should have different IDs\");\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(&ids[0]),\n        \"First ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(&ids[1]),\n        \"Second ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_preserves_existing_header() {\n    // If a request already has X-Request-Id, it should be preserved\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an existing X-Request-Id header\n    let existing_id = \"deadbeef\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .header(\"x-request-id\", existing_id)\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // The response should have the same X-Request-Id\n    let response_id = response.headers().get(\"x-request-id\").unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(response_id, existing_id, \"Should preserve existing X-Request-Id\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_invalid_header_is_replaced() {\n    // If a request has an invalid X-Request-Id (not 8 hex chars),\n    // the middleware should generate a new one\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an INVALID X-Request-Id header (wrong length)\n    let invalid_id = \"not-8-chars\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .header(\"x-request-id\", invalid_id)\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // The response should have a VALID 8-char hex ID (replaced)\n    let response_id = response.headers().get(\"x-request-id\").unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert_ne!(response_id, invalid_id, \"Should replace invalid X-Request-Id\");\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(response_id),\n        \"Replaced ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// P7.5.c Trace correlation: request_id appears in all log lines\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Custom writer to capture log output for testing\nstruct CaptureWriter {\n    logs: Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>,\n}\n\nimpl std::io::Write for CaptureWriter {\n    fn write(&mut self, buf: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<usize> {\n        if let Ok(s) = std::str::from_utf8(buf) {\n            self.logs.lock().unwrap().push(s.to_string());\n        }\n        Ok(buf.len())\n    }\n\n    fn flush(&mut self) -> std::io::Result<()> {\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_appears_in_all_log_lines_within_request() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Every log line inside a request must carry request_id=<id>\n    // This is achieved via tracing::Span with request_id recorded on span enter\n    // and tracing_subscriber::fmt().with_current_span(true)\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, telemetry_middleware, TelemetryState, RequestId, Metrics};\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n    use tracing_subscriber::{layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt};\n    use tracing::info;\n    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\n\n    // Capture logs into a vector\n    let captured_logs = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::<String>::new()));\n\n    // Create a custom layer that captures log events\n    let capture_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n        .json()\n        .with_target(true)\n        .with_current_span(true)  // This is the key: includes span fields on all events\n        .with_span_list(false)\n        .flatten_event(true)\n        .with_writer(std::sync::Mutex::new(CaptureWriter {\n            logs: captured_logs.clone(),\n        }));\n\n    // Initialize the subscriber with our capture layer\n    tracing_subscriber::registry()\n        .with(capture_layer)\n        .init();\n\n    // Build telemetry state\n    let config = MiroirConfig::default();\n    let metrics = Metrics::new(&config);\n    let telemetry = TelemetryState {\n        metrics: metrics.clone(),\n        pod_id: \"test-pod\".to_string(),\n    };\n\n    // Build app with full middleware stack\n    let app = axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n            // These log events should all inherit the request_id from the parent span\n            info!(message = \"handler started\", step = \"1\");\n            info!(message = \"processing request\", step = \"2\");\n            info!(message = \"handler completed\", step = \"3\");\n            \"ok\"\n        }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Make a request\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // Extract the request_id from the response header\n    let request_id = response.headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .unwrap()\n        .to_str()\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify all captured log lines contain the request_id\n    let logs = captured_logs.lock().unwrap();\n    let log_lines: Vec<&str> = logs.iter()\n        .flat_map(|s| s.lines())\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(\"message\"))  // Only look at actual log lines\n        .collect();\n\n    // We should have at least 3 log lines from the handler\n    assert!(\n        log_lines.len() >= 3,\n        \"Expected at least 3 log lines from handler, got: {}\",\n        log_lines.len()\n    );\n\n    // Every log line should contain the request_id\n    for line in &log_lines {\n        let json = parse_log_line(line).expect(&format!(\"Log line should be valid JSON: {}\", line));\n\n        // Verify request_id field exists and matches the response header\n        assert!(\n            json.get(\"request_id\").is_some(),\n            \"Log line missing request_id field: {}\",\n            line\n        );\n\n        let log_request_id = json[\"request_id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            log_request_id, request_id,\n            \"Log line request_id should match response header: {}\",\n            line\n        );\n    }\n}\n","numLines":630,"startLine":1,"totalLines":630}}}

   /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n66\t    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n67\t        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n68\t        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n69\t        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n70\t\n71\t        for resp in responses {\n72\t            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n73\t            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n74\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n75\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n76\t            }\n77\t        }\n78\t\n79\t        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n80\t            total_length / total_docs as f64\n81\t        } else {\n82\t            0.0\n83\t        };\n84\t\n85\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n86\t        let terms = term_df\n87\t            .into_iter()\n88\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n89\t                let idf = if df == 0 {\n90\t                    0.0\n91\t                } else {\n92\t                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n93\t                };\n94\t                (term, NetworkError(String),\n274\t}\n275\t\n276\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n277\tpub struct SearchRequest {\n278\t    pub index_uid: String,\n279\t    pub query: Option<String>,\n280\t    pub offset: IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n287\t    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n288\t}\n289\t\n290\timpl SearchRequest {\n291\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n292\t    ///\n293\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n294\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n295\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n296\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n297\t        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n298\t\n299\t        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n300\t        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n301\t\n302\t        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n303\t        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n304\t        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n305\t\n306\t        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n307\t        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n308\t\n309\t        // Ensure query is set\n310\t        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n311\t            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n312\t        }\n313\t\n314\t        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n315\t        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n316\t            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n317\t        }\n318\t\n319\t        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n320\t        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n321\t            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n322\t        }\n323\t\n324\t        body\n325\t    }\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\t#[derive(Debug)]\n329\tpub struct ScatterResult {\n330\t    pub shard_pages: fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n337\tpub fn plan_search_scatter(\n338\t    topology: &Topology,\n339\t    query_seq: u64,\n340\t    rf: usize,\n341\t    shard_count: u32,\n342\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n343\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n344\t\n345\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n346\t        Some(g) => g,\n347\t        None => {\n348\t            return ScatterPlan {\n349\t                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n350\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n351\t            };\n352\t        }\n353\t    };\n354\t\n355\t    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n356\t\n357\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n358\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n359\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n360\t        let selected = replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone();\n361\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n362\t    }\n363\t\n364\t    ScatterPlan {\n365\t        chosen_group,\n366\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n367\t        shard_to_node,\n368\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n369\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n370\t    }\n371\t}\n372\t\n373\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\n374\tpub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n375\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n376\t    client: &C,\n377\t    req: SearchRequest,\n378\t    topology: &Topology,\n379\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n380\t) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n381\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n382\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n383\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n384\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n385\t        }\n386\t    }\n387\t    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n388\t\n389\t    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n390\t    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n391\t    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n392\t\n393\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n394\t    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n395\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n396\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n397\t            None => {\n398\t                for shard_id in shards {\n399\t                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n400\t                }\n401\t                continue;\n402\t            }\n403\t        };\n404\t        let client_ref = client;\n405\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n406\t        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n407\t        let shard_count = shards.len();\n408\t        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n409\t        let span = info_span!(\n410\t            \"scatter_node\",\n411\t            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n412\t            address = %node.address,\n413\t            shard_count = shard_count,\n414\t        );\n415\t        tasks.push(async move {\n416\t            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n417\t            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n418\t        }.instrument(span));\n419\t    }\n420\t\n421\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n422\t\n423\t    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n424\t        match result {\n425\t            Ok(body) => {\n426\t                for _shard_id in shards {\n427\t                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n428\t                }\n429\t            }\n430\t            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n431\t                deadline_exceeded = true;\n432\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n433\t            }\n434\t            Err(e) => {\n435\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n436\t            }\n437\t        }\n438\t    }\n439\t\n440\t    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n441\t\n442\t    match policy {\n443\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n444\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n445\t                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n446\t            }\n447\t        }\n448\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n449\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n450\t            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n451\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n452\t                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n453\t                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n454\t\n455\t                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n456\t                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n457\t\n458\t                    // Try each other replica group\n459\t                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n460\t                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n461\t                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n462\t                        }\n463\t\n464\t                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n465\t                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n466\t                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n467\t                                continue;\n468\t                            }\n469\t\n470\t                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n471\t                            for node_id in replicas {\n472\t                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n473\t                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n474\t                                        Ok(body) => {\n475\t                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n476\t                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n477\t                                            break;\n478\t                                        }\n479\t                                        Err(_) => {\n480\t                                            continue; // Try next replica\n481\t                                        }\n482\t                                    }\n483\t                                }\n484\t                            }\n485\t\n486\t                            if fallback_succeeded {\n487\t                                break; // Found a working replica\n488\t                            }\n489\t                        }\n490\t                    }\n491\t\n492\t                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n493\t                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n494\t                    }\n495\t                }\n496\t\n497\t                // Merge fallback results\n498\t                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n499\t\n500\t                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n501\t                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n502\t                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n503\t                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n504\t                        shard_pages,\n505\t                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n506\t                        partial: false,\n507\t                        deadline_exceeded,\n508\t                    });\n509\t                } else {\n510\t                    // Some shards still failed\n511\t                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n512\t                }\n513\t            }\n514\t        }\n515\t    }\n516\t\n517\t    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n518\t}\n519\t\n520\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n521\tpub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n522\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n523\t    client: &C,\n524\t    req: SearchRequest,\n525\t    topology: &Topology,\n526\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n527\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n528\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n529\t    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n530\t\n531\t    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n532\t    if scatter_result.partial {\n533\t        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n534\t            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n535\t                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n536\t            });\n537\t        }\n538\t    }\n539\t\n540\t    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n541\t    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n542\t\n543\t    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n544\t        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n545\t        offset: req.offset,\n546\t        limit: req.limit,\n547\t        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n548\t        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n549\t        failed_shards,\n550\t    };\n551\t\n552\t    // Span for the merge operation\n553\t    let _span = info_span!(\n554\t        \"merge\",\n555\t        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n556\t        offset = req.offset,\n557\t        limit = req.limit,\n558\t    ).entered();\n559\t\n560\t    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n561\t}\n562\t\n563\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n564\t// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n565\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n566\t\n567\t/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\n568\tpub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n569\t    match query {\n570\t        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n571\t            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n572\t            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n573\t            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n574\t                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n575\t                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n576\t            }\n577\t            terms\n578\t        }\n579\t        _ => Vec::new(),\n580\t    }\n581\t}\n582\t\n583\t/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n584\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\n585\tpub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n586\t    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n587\t    client: &C,\n588\t    req: &PreflightRequest,\n589\t    topology: &Topology,\n590\t) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n591\t    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n592\t        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n593\t    }\n594\t\n595\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n596\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n597\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n598\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n599\t        }\n600\t    }\n601\t\n602\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n603\t    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n604\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n605\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n606\t            None => continue,\n607\t        };\n608\t        let client_ref = client;\n609\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n610\t        let nid = node_id.clone();\n611\t        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n612\t    }\n613\t\n614\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n615\t    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n616\t    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n620\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n621\tpub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n622\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n623\t    client: &C,\n624\t    req: SearchRequest,\n625\t    topology: &Topology,\n626\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n627\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n628\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n629\t    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n630\t        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n631\t        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n632\t        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n633\t    };\n634\t    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n635\t    let mut search_req = req;\n636\t    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n637\t    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n638\t}\n639\t\n640\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n641\t// Mock client\n642\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n643\t\n644\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n645\tpub struct MockNodeClient {\n646\t    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n647\t    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n648\t    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n649\t    pub delay_ms: u64,\n650\t}\n651\t\n652\timpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n653\t    async fn search_node(\n654\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n655\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n656\t        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n657\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n658\t        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n659\t            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n660\t        }))\n661\t    }\n662\t\n663\t    async fn preflight_node(\n664\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n665\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n666\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n667\t        Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n668\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n669\t        }))\n670\t    }\n671\t\n672\t    async fn write_documents(\n673\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n674\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n675\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n676\t        Ok(WriteResponse {\n677\t            success: true,\n678\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n679\t            message: None,\n680\t            code: None,\n681\t            error_type: None,\n682\t        })\n683\t    }\n684\t\n685\t    async fn delete_documents(\n686\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n687\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n688\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n689\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n690\t            success: true,\n691\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n692\t            message: None,\n693\t            code: None,\n694\t            error_type: None,\n695\t        })\n696\t    }\n697\t\n698\t    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n699\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n700\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n701\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n702\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n703\t            success: true,\n704\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n705\t            message: None,\n706\t            code: None,\n707\t            error_type: None,\n708\t        })\n709\t    }\n710\t\n711\t    fn get_task_status(\n712\t        &self,\n713\t        node: &NodeId,\n714\t        _address: &str,\n715\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n716\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n717\t        let node = node.clone();\n718\t        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n719\t        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n720\t        async move {\n721\t            if let Some(err) = error {\n722\t                return Err(err);\n723\t            }\n724\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n725\t                task_uid,\n726\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n727\t                error: None,\n728\t                error_type: None,\n729\t            })\n730\t        }\n731\t    }\n732\t}\n733\t\n734\t#[cfg(test)]\n735\tmod tests {\n736\t    use super::*;\n737\t    use crate::topology::{Node, assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n860\t    }\n861\t\n862\t    #[test]\n863\t    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n864\t        let resp = vec![\n865\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n866\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 60.0, 100000);\n870\t        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n871\t        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n872\t    }\n873\t\n874\t    #[test]\n875\t    fn }\n880\t\n881\t    #[tokio::test]\n882\t    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n883\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n884\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n885\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n886\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n887\t            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n888\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n889\t        });\n890\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n891\t            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n892\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n893\t        });\n894\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n895\t            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n896\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n897\t        });\n898\t        let req = PreflightRequest { index_uid: \"test\".into(), terms: vec![\"search\".into()], filter: None };\n899\t        let g = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n900\t        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n901\t        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n902\t    }\n903\t\n904\t    #[tokio::test]\n905\t    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n906\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n907\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n908\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n909\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n910\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n911\t            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n912\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n913\t        });\n914\t        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n915\t        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n916\t        assert!(!r.degraded);\n917\t        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n918\t    }\n919\t\n920\t    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n921\t    ///\n922\t    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n923\t    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n924\t    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n925\t    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n926\t    ///\n927\t    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n928\t    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n929\t    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n930\t    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n931\t    ///\n932\t    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n933\t    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n934\t    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n935\t    ///\n936\t    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n937\t    #[tokio::test]\n938\t    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n939\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n940\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n941\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n942\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n943\t\n944\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n945\t\n946\t        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n947\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n948\t\n949\t        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n950\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n951\t            total_docs: 1000,\n952\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n953\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n954\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n955\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n956\t            ]),\n957\t        });\n958\t\n959\t        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n960\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n961\t            total_docs: 100_000,\n962\t            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n963\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n964\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n965\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n966\t            ]),\n967\t        });\n968\t\n969\t        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n970\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n971\t            total_docs: 10,\n972\t            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n973\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n974\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n975\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n976\t            ]),\n977\t        });\n978\t\n979\t        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n980\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n981\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n982\t            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n983\t            filter: None,\n984\t        };\n985\t\n986\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n987\t\n988\t        // Verify global aggregation\n989\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n990\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n991\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n992\t\n993\t        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n994\t        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n995\t        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n996\t        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n997\t        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n998\t\n999\t        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n1000\t        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1001\t        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n1002\t        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n1003\t\n1004\t        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n1005\t        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n1006\t        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n1007\t        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n1008\t        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1009\t        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1010\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n1011\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n1012\t    }\n1013\t\n1014\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n1015\t    #[tokio::test]\n1016\t    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n1017\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1018\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n1019\t        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1020\t\n1021\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1022\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1023\t            terms: vec![],\n1024\t            filter: None,\n1025\t        };\n1026\t\n1027\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1028\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n1029\t        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n1030\t    }\n1031\t\n1032\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n1033\t    #[tokio::test]\n1034\t    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n1035\t        let mut topo = Scatter orchestration: fan-out logic and covering set builder.\n\nuse crate::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse tracing::{instrument, info_span, Instrument};\nuse crate::merger::{MergeInput, MergedSearchResult, MergeStrategy, ShardHitPage};\nuse crate::router::{covering_set, query_group};\nuse crate::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Scatter plan: the exact shard→node mapping for a search query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScatterPlan {\n    pub chosen_group: u32,\n    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\n    pub shard_to_node: HashMap<u32, NodeId>,\n    pub deadline_ms: u32,\n    pub hedging_eligible: bool,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// §15 OP#4: Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch pattern)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Per-term document frequency from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n}\n\n/// Preflight request: gather term-frequency statistics from a shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub terms: Vec<String>,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a shard's preflight query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightResponse {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub term_stats: HashMap<String, TermStats>,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global term statistics after coordinator aggregation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalTermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n    pub idf: f64,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global IDF data computed at the coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalIdf {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub terms: HashMap<String, GlobalTermStats>,\n}\n\nimpl GlobalIdf {\n    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n        for resp in responses {\n            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n            total_length / total_docs as f64\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        let n = total_docs as f64;\n        let terms = term_df\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(term, df)| {\n                let idf = if df == 0 {\n                    0.0\n                } else {\n                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n                };\n                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Write path: document operations (P2.2)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Request to add/replace documents on a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub documents: Vec<Value>,\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node's document write operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteResponse {\n    pub success: bool,\n    pub task_uid: Option<u64>,\n    pub message: Option<String>,\n    pub code: Option<String>,\n    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request to delete documents by IDs.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DeleteByIdsRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub ids: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request to delete all documents matching a filter.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DeleteByFilterRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub filter: Value,\n}\n\n/// Response from a delete operation.\npub type DeleteResponse = WriteResponse;\n\n/// Request to get task status from a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskStatusRequest {\n    /// The task UID to query\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node's task status query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskStatusResponse {\n    /// The task UID\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n    /// Current task status\n    pub status: String,\n    /// Error message if failed\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n    /// Error type if failed\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl TaskStatusResponse {\n    /// Convert Meilisearch status string to NodeTaskStatus.\n    pub fn to_node_status(&self) -> crate::task::NodeTaskStatus {\n        match self.status.as_str() {\n            \"enqueued\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            \"processing\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing,\n            \"succeeded\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded,\n            \"failed\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed,\n            _ => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// HTTP client for communicating with a Meilisearch node.\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\npub trait NodeClient: Send + Sync {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError>;\n\n    /// Execute a preflight request (OP#4 global-IDF phase).\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(PreflightResponse { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() })\n    }\n\n    /// Write documents to a node (add/replace).\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(WriteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Get task status from a node.\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        async move {\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid: _request.task_uid,\n                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Delete documents by IDs from a node.\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Delete all documents matching a filter from a node.\n    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum NodeError {\n    Timeout,\n    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n    NetworkError(String),\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SearchRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: Option<String>,\n    pub offset: usize,\n    pub limit: usize,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub ranking_score: bool,\n    pub body: Value,\n    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n}\n\nimpl SearchRequest {\n    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n    ///\n    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n    /// data to apply pagination.\n    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n\n        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n\n        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n\n        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n\n        // Ensure query is set\n        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n        }\n\n        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n        }\n\n        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n        }\n\n        body\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct ScatterResult {\n    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n    pub partial: bool,\n    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\npub fn plan_search_scatter(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n) -> ScatterPlan {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n        Some(g) => g,\n        None => {\n            return ScatterPlan {\n                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n        let selected = replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone();\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n    }\n\n    ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    }\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\npub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n\n    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n            None => {\n                for shard_id in shards {\n                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n        };\n        let client_ref = client;\n        let req_clone = req.clone();\n        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n        let shard_count = shards.len();\n        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n        let span = info_span!(\n            \"scatter_node\",\n            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n            address = %node.address,\n            shard_count = shard_count,\n        );\n        tasks.push(async move {\n            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n        }.instrument(span));\n    }\n\n    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n\n    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n        match result {\n            Ok(body) => {\n                for _shard_id in shards {\n                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n                }\n            }\n            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n                deadline_exceeded = true;\n                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n\n    match policy {\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n            }\n        }\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n\n                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n\n                    // Try each other replica group\n                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n                        }\n\n                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n                                continue;\n                            }\n\n                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n                            for node_id in replicas {\n                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                                        Ok(body) => {\n                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n                                            break;\n                                        }\n                                        Err(_) => {\n                                            continue; // Try next replica\n                                        }\n                                    }\n                                }\n                            }\n\n                            if fallback_succeeded {\n                                break; // Found a working replica\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Merge fallback results\n                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n\n                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n                        shard_pages,\n                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n                        partial: false,\n                        deadline_exceeded,\n                    });\n                } else {\n                    // Some shards still failed\n                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\npub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n\n    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n    if scatter_result.partial {\n        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n\n    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n        offset: req.offset,\n        limit: req.limit,\n        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n        failed_shards,\n    };\n\n    // Span for the merge operation\n    let _span = info_span!(\n        \"merge\",\n        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n        offset = req.offset,\n        limit = req.limit,\n    ).entered();\n\n    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\npub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n    match query {\n        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n            }\n            terms\n        }\n        _ => Vec::new(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\npub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: &PreflightRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n    }\n\n    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n            None => continue,\n        };\n        let client_ref = client;\n        let req_clone = req.clone();\n        let nid = node_id.clone();\n        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n    }\n\n    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n}\n\n/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\npub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n    };\n    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n    let mut search_req = req;\n    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Mock client\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct MockNodeClient {\n    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n    pub delay_ms: u64,\n}\n\nimpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(WriteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let node = node.clone();\n        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n        async move {\n            if let Some(err) = error {\n                return Err(err);\n            }\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid,\n                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    fn make_test_topology() -> Topology {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            let mut node = Node::new(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")), format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"), rg);\n            node.status = crate::topology::NodeStatus::Active;\n            topo.add_node(node);\n        }\n        topo\n    }\n\n    fn make_req() -> SearchRequest {\n        SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(), query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 0, limit: 10, filter: None, facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false, body: serde_json::json!({}), global_idf: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_pure_function() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan =
