\"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        assert!(scatter_gather_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap().degraded);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_query_terms() {\n        assert_eq!(extract_query_terms(&Some(\"hello world hello\".into())), vec![\"hello\", \"world\"]);\n        assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let resp = vec![\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 60.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 4500 })]) },\n        ];\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&resp);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_empty() {\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&[]);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(g.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n        });\n        let req = PreflightRequest { index_uid: \"test\".into(), terms: vec![\"search\".into()], filter: None };\n        let g = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n        });\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.degraded);\n        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n    ///\n    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    ///\n    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n\n        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 1000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100_000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 10,\n            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify global aggregation\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n\n        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Node 0 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 1 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 2 fails\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"test\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        // Should aggregate from successful nodes only\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 80000);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"test\").unwrap().df, 8000);\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n            offset: 10,\n            limit: 20,\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n\n        // query must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n\n        // filter must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")));\n\n        // facets must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"facets\"), Some(&serde_json::json!([\"category\", \"tags\"])));\n\n        // custom body field must be preserved\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"custom\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"field\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body works with minimal request.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_minimal_request() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: None,\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: true, // Client requested scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit = 10\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(10)));\n\n        // offset must be 0\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body handles large offset/limit correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_pagination() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 100,\n            limit: 50,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false,\n            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit = 150 (fetch enough for coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(150)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when primary group has failed nodes.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_on_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: 2 nodes (healthy fallback targets)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16); // query_seq=0 → group 0\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up responses: all shards on group 1 nodes return valid data\n        let response_1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        });\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), response_1.clone());\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), response_1);\n\n        // All nodes in group 0 fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, shards should succeed via group 1\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should have succeeded via fallback (group 1)\n        assert!(!result.partial, \"Fallback should have succeeded\");\n        assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"No shards should have failed after fallback\");\n        assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have shard pages from fallback\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when both groups are down.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_all_groups_down() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // All nodes fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, should still fail when all groups are down\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.partial, \"Should be partial when all groups fail\");\n        assert!(!result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"Should have failed shards\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that partial policy does NOT use fallback.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_partial_policy_no_fallback() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Group 1 nodes are healthy but partial policy shouldn't use them\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"fallback-doc\"}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n        }));\n\n        // Group 0 nodes fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With partial policy, should NOT use fallback\n assert!(!result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"Should have failed shards\");\n        // Should NOT have any successful pages (fallback not used)\n        assert!(result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Partial policy should not use fallback\");\n    }\n}\n","numLines":1262,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1262}}}

fn validate_origin(\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n    allowed_origins: &[String],\n    is_same_origin_by_default: bool,\n) -> OriginVerdict {\n    // Try Origin header first (preferred for POST/DELETE/PUT)\n    let origin = headers.get(\"origin\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok());\n\n    // Fall back to Referer header (for navigational requests)\n    let referer = origin.or_else(|| headers.get(\"referer\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()));\n\n    let provided_origin = match referer {\n        Some(o) => o,\n        None => {\n            // No Origin or Referer header - for same-origin requests, this is acceptable\n            return if is_same_origin_by_default {\n                OriginVerdict::Missing\n            } else {\n                OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Strip path from Referer to get origin\n    let provided_origin = if let Some(ref_hdr) = headers.get(\"referer\") {\n        if let Ok(ref_val) = ref_hdr.to_str() {\n            // Find the first '/' after \"https://\" (skip the first 8 chars: \"https://\")\n            if let Some(idx) = ref_val.chars().enumerate().skip(8).find(|(_, c)| *c == '/').map(|(i, _)| i) {\n                &ref_val[..idx]\n            } else {\n                ref_val\n            }\n        } else {\n            provided_origin\n        }\n    } else {\n        provided_origin\n    };\n\n    // Check against allowed origins\n    for allowed in allowed_origins {\n        // Special \"same-origin\" value - compare against Host header\n        if allowed == \"same-origin\" {\n            if let Some(host) = headers.get(\"host\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()) {\n                // Construct origin from scheme (https) and host\n                let same_origin = format!(\"https://{}\", host);\n                if provided_origin == same_origin || provided_origin == host {\n                    return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n                }\n            }\n        } else if allowed == \"*\" {\n            // Wildcard allows any origin\n            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n        } else if provided_origin == allowed {\n            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n        }\n    }\n\n    OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// CSP header builder (plan §9)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Build a CSP header value by merging base template with overrides.\n/// Overrides are merged additively - they never replace the base template.\npub fn build_csp_header(\n    base_template: &str,\n    overrides: &miroir_core::config::CspOverridesConfig,\n) -> String {\n    let mut directives: Vec<(String, Vec<String>)> = base_template\n        .split(';')\n        .map(|s| s.trim())\n        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n        .map(|directive| {\n            let parts: Vec<&str> = directive.splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n            if parts.len() == 2 {\n                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![parts[1].to_string()])\n            } else {\n                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![])\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Helper to merge overrides into a directive\n    let merge_into = |directives: &mut Vec<(String, Vec<String>)>,\n                      name: &str,\n                      values: &[String]| {\n        if values.is_empty() {\n            return;\n        }\n        let name_lower = name.to_lowercase();\n        if let Some(entry) = directives.iter_mut().find(|(n, _)| n == &name_lower) {\n            // Append to existing directive\n            entry.1.extend(values.iter().cloned());\n            entry.1.dedup(); // Remove duplicates\n        } else {\n            // Add new directive\n            directives.push((name_lower, values.to_vec()));\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Merge each override category\n    merge_into(&mut directives, \"script-src\", &overrides.script_src);\n    merge_into(&mut directives, \"img-src\", &overrides.img_src);\n    merge_into(&mut directives, \"connect-src\", &overrides.connect_src);\n\n    // Rebuild CSP string\n    directives\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(name, values)| {\n            if values.is_empty() {\n                name\n            } else {\n                format!(\"{} {}\", name, values.join(\" \"))\n            }\n        })\n        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n        .join(\"; \")\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Dispatch verdict\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Result of the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum AuthVerdict {\n    /// Request is dispatch-exempt (rule 0); handler decides auth.\n    Exempt,\n    /// Authenticated with the given token kind.\n    Authenticated(TokenKind),\n    /// Bearer token looked like a JWT but failed validation (rule 1).\n    JwtInvalid,\n    /// JWT was signature-valid but scope was insufficient (rule 1).\n    JwtScopeDenied,\n    /// No matching key / missing Authorization (rule 4).\n    InvalidAuth,\n}\n\n/// Which key or token type satisfied authentication.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum TokenKind {\n    MasterKey,\n    AdminKey,\n    /// JWT validated against primary or previous secret.\n    Jwt,\n    /// Admin session cookie — sealed session ID validated against task store.\n    AdminSession,\n}\n\nimpl AuthVerdict {\n    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, AuthVerdict::Exempt | AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_))\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt check\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Returns true when `(method, path)` is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list\n/// (plan §5 rule 5). Exempt endpoints run their handler directly; rules 1–4\n/// are never consulted.\npub fn is_dispatch_exempt(method: &Method, path: &str) -> bool {\n    // `GET /health` — unauthenticated liveness probe (Meilisearch-compatible)\n    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/health\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /version` — unauthenticated version endpoint (Meilisearch-compatible)\n    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/version\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /_miroir/ready` — unauthenticated readiness probe (plan §10)\n    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/_miroir/ready\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch\n    if method == Method::GET {\n        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/\") {\n            // Must have at least one path segment after the prefix\n            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains(\"//\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body\n    if method == Method::POST && path == \"/_miroir/admin/login\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per search_ui.auth.mode\n    if method == Method::GET {\n        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/\") {\n            let segments: Vec<&str> = rest.split('/').collect();\n            if segments.len() == 2 && segments[1] == \"session\" && !segments[0].is_empty() {\n                return true;","numLines":200,"startLine":300,"totalLines":1766}}}

fn test_request_id_appears_in_all_log_lines_within_request() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Every log line inside a request must carry request_id=<id>\n    // This is achieved via tracing::Span with P7.5 Structured JSON logging + request IDs + trace correlation tests.\n//!\n//! Tests for plan §10 structured logging format:\n//! - JSON-per-line parseable by jq\n//! - request_id on every log line (same as X-Request-Id header)\n//! - No PII (API keys, document content, query strings) in logs\n//! - Log volume: 2 INFO entries per search request (middleware + search handler)\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse axum::{\n    extract::Request,\n    body::Body,\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Router,\n};\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\n\n/// Helper: parse a JSON log line and extract fields\nfn parse_log_line(line: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {\n    serde_json::from_str(line).ok()\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a string is a valid 8-char hex request ID (from RequestId::new)\nfn is_valid_request_id(s: &str) -> bool {\n    s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit())\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a line contains PII patterns\nfn contains_pii(line: &str) -> bool {\n    let lower = line.to_lowercase();\n    // Check for API key patterns\n    if lower.contains(\"api_key\") || lower.contains(\"apikey\") || lower.contains(\"api-key\") {\n        // Allow if it's just \"api_key_hash\" or similar\n        if !lower.contains(\"hash\") && !lower.contains(\"uid\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    // Check for master keys\n    if lower.contains(\"master\") && (lower.contains(\"key\") || lower.contains(\"secret\")) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for JWT tokens\n    if lower.contains(\"eyj\") && lower.len() > 100 {\n        // JWT base64 pattern\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for document content patterns (long text blocks in logs)\n    if line.len() > 5000 {\n        // Very long log line might contain document content\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for raw query strings (both URL-style q= and JSON-style \"q\":)\n    if (lower.contains(\"q=\") || lower.contains(\"\\\"q\\\":\")) && line.len() > 200 {\n        // But not if the value is explicitly redacted\n        if !lower.contains(\"\\\"[redacted]\\\"\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    false\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 1: jq parses every log line\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_json_logs_parseable_by_jq() {\n    // Sample log entry in the expected format\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"GET /indexes/products/search 200\",\n        \"request_id\": \"abc123\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"miroir-proxy-7f8d9c6d5-abc12\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42,\n        \"status\": 200,\n        \"method\": \"GET\",\n        \"path_template\": \"/indexes/{uid}/search\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log);\n    assert!(parsed.is_some(), \"Sample log should parse as JSON\");\n\n    let log_obj = parsed.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"level\"], \"info\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"target\"], \"miroir.request\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"request_id\"], \"abc123\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"duration_ms\"], 42);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_format_in_logs() {\n    // Verify request_id appears in JSON logs\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"search completed\",\n        \"request_id\": \"a1b2c3d4\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"test-pod\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log).unwrap();\n    let request_id = parsed[\"request_id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n\n    // Request IDs should be 8 hex chars (from RequestId::new())\n    assert_eq!(request_id.len(), 8);\n    assert!(request_id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 2: request_id traceable across pods\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_extraction_from_logs() {\n    let logs = vec![\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"message\":\"GET /search 200\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.001Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"node_id\":\"node-1\",\"message\":\"node call started\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.010Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"index\":\"products\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    // Extract all logs with request_id = \"abc12345\"\n    let target_id = \"abc12345\";\n    let matching_logs: Vec<_> = logs\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(&format!(\"\\\"request_id\\\":\\\"{}\\\"\", target_id)))\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(matching_logs.len(), 3, \"Should find all 3 log lines for the request\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 3: No PII in logs\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_api_keys_in_logs() {\n    // These patterns should NOT appear in logs\n    let pii_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key\": \"master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"bearer\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"authorization\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in pii_patterns {\n        assert!(\n            contains_pii(pattern),\n            \"Pattern should be flagged as PII: {}\",\n            pattern\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Hashed versions are OK\n    let safe_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key_hash\": \"a1b2c3d4\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"key_uid\": \"12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"tenant_id\": \"tenant-123\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in safe_patterns {\n        assert!(\n            !contains_pii(pattern),\n            \"Hashed pattern should NOT be flagged as PII: {}\",\n            pattern\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_query_strings_in_logs() {\n    // Raw Meilisearch query content should not be logged\n    // The contains_pii function checks for long q= patterns (user queries)\n    // Line must be >200 chars total and contain \"q=\" to trigger PII detection\n    let padding = \"x\".repeat(200);\n    let bad_log = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = 'test@example.com'\", \"padding\": \"{}\"}}\"#, padding);\n    assert!(contains_pii(&bad_log), \"Long query string should be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Redacted queries are OK\n    let good_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"query\": \"[redacted]\", \"q\": \"[redacted]\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(good_log), \"Redacted query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Short queries are OK (e.g., just a word or two)\n    let short_query = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"test\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(short_query), \"Short query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Long line without q= is OK\n    let long_safe = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing\", \"data\": \"{}\"}}\"#, \"x\".repeat(200));\n    assert!(!contains_pii(&long_safe), \"Long line without q= should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_document_content_in_logs() {\n    // Large JSON blocks likely contain document content\n    let large_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"indexing document\", \"doc\": {\"#;\n    let large_log_padded = format!(\"{}{}\", large_log, \"x\".repeat(5000));\n    assert!(contains_pii(&large_log_padded), \"Very long log line should be flagged as potential PII\");\n\n    // Normal log lines are OK\n    let normal_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"indexed 100 documents\", \"count\": 100}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(normal_log), \"Normal log line should NOT be flagged\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_response_header() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Every response includes X-Request-Id: <8-char hex>\n    use axum::routing::get;\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::request_id_middleware;\n\n    async fn handler() -> &'static str {\n        \"ok\"\n    }\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(handler))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    let headers = response.headers();\n    let request_id = headers.get(\"x-request-id\");\n    assert!(request_id.is_some(), \"X-Request-Id header should be present\");\n\n    let id_str = request_id.unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(id_str),\n        \"X-Request-Id should be 8 hex chars, got: {}\",\n        id_str\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_extension_accessible() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>() works from any handler\n    use        );\n            \"ok\"\n        }))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n Extension};\n    use axum::extract::State as AxumState;\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n    use std::sync::Mutex;\n\n    let captured_ids = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::<String>::new()));\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>, AxumState(ids): AxumState<Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>>| async move {\n                ids.lock().unwrap().push(id.as_str().to_string());\n                \"ok\"\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware))\n        .with_state(captured_ids.clone());\n\n    // Make two requests consecutively\n    for _ in 0..2 {\n        let _ = app\n            .clone()\n            .oneshot(\n                Request::builder()\n                    .uri(\"/test\")\n                    .body(Body::empty())\n                    .unwrap(),\n            )\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    let ids = captured_ids.lock().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(ids.len(), 2, \"Should have captured 2 request IDs\");\n    assert_ne!(ids[0], Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an existing X-Request-Id header\n    let existing_id = \"deadbeef\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n test_request_id_invalid_header_is_replaced() {\n    // If a request has an invalid X-Request-Id (not 8 hex chars),\n    // the middleware should generate a new one\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n a request with an INVALID X-Request-Id header (wrong length)\n    let invalid_id = \"not-8-chars\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n is_valid_request_id(response_id),\n        \"Replaced ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// P7.5.c Trace correlation: request_id appears in all log lines\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Custom writer to capture log output for testing\nstruct CaptureWriter {\n    logs: Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>,\n}\n\nimpl std::io::Write for CaptureWriter {\n    fn write(&mut self, buf: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<usize> {\n        if let Ok(s) = std::str::from_utf8(buf) {\n            self.logs.lock().unwrap().push(s.to_string());\n        }\n        Ok(buf.len())\n    }\n\n    fn flush(&mut self) -> std::io::Result<()> {\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_appears_in_all_log_lines_within_request() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Every log line inside a request must carry request_id=<id>\n    // This is achieved via tracing::Span with request_id recorded on span enter\n    // and tracing_subscriber::fmt().with_current_span(true)\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, telemetry_middleware, TelemetryState, RequestId, Metrics};\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n    use tracing_subscriber::{layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt};\n    use tracing::info;\n    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\n\n    // Capture logs into a vector\n    let captured_logs = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::<String>::new()));\n\n    // Create a custom layer that captures log events\n    let capture_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n        .json()\n        .with_target(true)\n        .with_current_span(true)  // This is the key: includes span fields on all events\n        .with_span_list(false)\n        .flatten_event(true)\n        .with_writer(std::sync::Mutex::new(CaptureWriter {\n            logs: captured_logs.clone(),\n        }));\n\n    // Initialize the subscriber with our capture layer\n    tracing_subscriber::registry()\n        .with(capture_layer)\n        .init();\n\n    // Build telemetry state\n    let config = MiroirConfig::default();\n    let metrics = Metrics::new(&config);\n    let telemetry = TelemetryState {\n        metrics: metrics.clone(),\n        pod_id: \"test-pod\".to_string(),\n    };\n\n    // Build app with full middleware stack\n    // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n    // request_id_middleware must run BEFORE telemetry_middleware.\n    // To achieve this, telemetry_middleware layer() call comes BEFORE request_id_middleware.\n    let app = axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(|Extension(_id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n            // These log events should all inherit the request_id from the parent span\n            info!(message = \"handler started\", step = \"1\");\n            info!(message = \"processing request\", step = \"2\");\n            info!(message = \"handler completed\", step = \"3\");\n            \"ok\"\n        }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Make a request\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // Extract the request_id from the response header\n    let request_id = response.headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .unwrap()\n        .to_str()\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify all captured log lines contain the request_id\n    let logs = captured_logs.lock().unwrap();\n    let log_lines: Vec<&str> = logs.iter()\n        .flat_map(|s| s.lines())\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(\"message\"))  // Only look at actual log lines\n        .collect();\n\n    // We should have at least 3 log lines from the handler\n    assert!(\n        log_lines.len() >= 3,\n        \"Expected at least 3 log lines from handler, got: {}\",\n        log_lines.len()\n    );\n\n    // Every log line should contain the request_id\n    for line in &log_lines {\n        let json = parse_log_line(line).expect(&format!(\"Log line should be valid JSON: {}\", line));\n\n        // Verify request_id field exists and matches the response header\n        assert!(\n            json.get(\"request_id\").is_some(),\n            \"Log line missing request_id field: {}\",\n            line\n        );\n\n        let log_request_id = json[\"request_id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            log_request_id, request_id,\n            \"Log line request_id should match response header: {}\",\n            line\n        );\n Structured logging, request IDs, and Prometheus metrics middleware.\n\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Request, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n    Router,\n    routing::get,\n};\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse prometheus::{\n    Counter, CounterVec, Encoder, Gauge, GaugeVec, Histogram, HistogramOpts, HistogramVec, Opts,\n    Registry, TextEncoder,\n};\nuse tracing::info_span;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\nuse hex;\nuse std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n\n/// Request ID wrapper type for storing in axum Request extensions.\n///\n/// This is a newtype wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct RequestId(pub String);\n\nimpl RequestId {\n    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n    ///\n    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n        hasher.write(bytes);\n        let hash = hasher.finish();\n        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub async fn request_id_middleware(\n    req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    let mut req = req;\n    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n\n    // Process the request\n    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n\n/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct TelemetryState {\n    pub metrics: Metrics,\n    pub pod_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl TelemetryState {\n    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n        Self { metrics, pod_id }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Global metrics registry shared across all middleware instances.\npub struct Metrics {\n    registry: Registry,\n\n    // ── Request metrics ──\n    request_duration: HistogramVec,\n    requests_total: CounterVec,\n    requests_in_flight: Gauge,\n\n pub fn new(config: &MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n        let registry = Registry::new();\n\n        // ── Request metrics ──\n        let request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\", \"Request latency in seconds\")\n                .buckets(vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0]),\n            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create request_duration histogram\");\n\n        let requests_total = CounterVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests\"),\n            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create requests_total counter\");\n\n        let requests_in_flight = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\", \"Number of requests currently being processed\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create requests_in_flight gauge\");\n\n        // ── Node health metrics ──\n        let node_healthy = GaugeVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_healthy\", \"Health status of backend nodes (1=healthy, 0=unhealthy)\"),\n            &[\"node_id\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create node_healthy gauge\");\n\n        let node_request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_node_request_duration_seconds\", \"Latency of individual node requests\")\n                .buckets(vec![0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0]),\n            &[\"node_id\", \"operation\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create node_request_duration histogram\");\n\n        let node_errors = CounterVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_errors_total\", \"Number of errors from backend nodes\"),\n            &[\"node_id\", \"error_type\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create node_errors counter\");\n\n        // ── Shard metrics ──\n        let shard_coverage = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_coverage\", \"Fraction of shards with at least one healthy replica\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create shard_coverage gauge\");\n\n        let degraded_shards = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_degraded_shards_total\", \"Number of shards with reduced replica availability\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create degraded_shards gauge\");\n\n        let shard_distribution = GaugeVec::new(\n .expect(\"failed to create scatter_fan_out_size histogram\");\n\n        let scatter_partial_responses = Counter::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total\", \"Number reg!(owned_shards_count);\n\n        // ── Admin session sealing metrics (always present) ──\n        let admin_session_key_generated = Gauge::with_opts(\n via logout\")\n        ).expect(\"create admin_session_revoked_total\");\n        reg!(admin_session_key_generated);\n        reg!(admin_session_revoked_total);\n\n        Self {\n            registry,\n            request_duration,\n            requests_total,\n            requests_in_flight,\n            node_healthy,\n            node_request_duration,\n            node_errors,\n            shard_coverage,\n            degraded_shards,\n            shard_distribution,\n            task_processing_age,\n            tasks_total,\n            task_registry_size,\n admin_session_revoked_total,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn encode_metrics(&self) -> Result<String, prometheus::Error> {\n        let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n        })?)\n    }\n\n    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n    }\n\n    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n///\n/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\npub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n\n    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n    let hash = hasher.finish();\n\n    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n}\n\n/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\npub trait RequestIdExt {\n    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n}\n\nimpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n    }\n\n    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n///\n/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n/// is accurately decremented.\nstruct InFlightGuard {\n    metrics: Metrics,\n}\n\nimpl InFlightGuard {\n    fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n        tracing::trace!(requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(), \"request started\");\n        Self { metrics }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n    fn drop(&mut self) {\n        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n        tracing::trace!(requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(), \"request completed\");\n    }\n}\n\n/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n///\n/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\nfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n    request\n        .extensions()\n        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n        .to_string()\n}\n\n/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n///\n/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\npub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n\n    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get_request_id()\n        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n\n    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n    let span = info_span!(\n        \"request\",\n        request_id = %request_id,\n        pod_id = %pod_id,\n        method = %method,\n        path_template = %path_template,\n    );\n\n    let _guard = span.enter();\n\n    // Track in-flight requests\n    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone());\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    drop(in_flight);\n\n    let status = response.status();\n    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n    let duration = start.elapsed();\n\n    // Record Prometheus metrics\n    metrics\n        .request_duration\n        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n    metrics\n        .requests_total\n        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n        .inc();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n    if status.is_server_error() {\n        tracing::error!(\n            target: \"miroir.request\",\n            pod_id = %pod_id,\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            message = %message,\n            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n            status = status_u16,\n            method = %method,\n            path_template = %path_template,\n        );\n    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n        tracing::warn!(\n            target: \"miroir.request\",\n            pod_id = %pod_id,\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            message = %message,\n            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n            status = status_u16,\n            method = %method,\n            path_template = %path_template,\n        );\n    } else {\n        tracing::info!(\n            target: \"miroir.request\",\n            pod_id = %pod_id,\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            message = %message,\n            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n            status = status_u16,\n            method = %method,\n            path_template = %path_template,\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n    let mut response = response;\n    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n        }\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\npub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n}\n\n/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\nasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> String {\n    match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\nimpl Metrics {\n    // ── Scatter-gather ──\n\n    pub fn record_scatter_fan_out(&self, size: u64) {\n        self.scatter_fan_out_size.observe(size as f64);\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_scatter_partial_responses(&self) {\n        self.scatter_partial_responses.inc();\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_scatter_retries(&self) {\n        self.scatter_retries.inc();\n    }\n\n    // ── Node health ──\n\n    pub fn set_node_healthy(&self, node_id: &str, healthy: bool) {\n        self.node_healthy.with_label_values(&[node_id]).set(if healthy { 1.0 } else { 0.0 });\n    }\n\n    pub fn record_node_request_duration(&self, node_id: &str, operation: &str, duration_secs: f64) {\n        self.node_request_duration.with_label_values(&[node_id, operation]).observe(duration_secs);\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_node_errors(&self, node_id: &str, error_type: &str) {\n        self.node_errors.with_label_values(&[node_id, error_type]).inc();\n    }\n\n    // ── Shards ──\n\n    pub fn set_shard_coverage(&self, coverage: f64) {\n        self.shard_coverage.set(coverage);\n    }\n\n    pub fn set_degraded_shards(&self, count: f64) {\n        self.degraded_shards.set(count);\n    }\n\n    pub fn set_shard_distribution(&self, node_id: &str, count: f64) {\n        self.shard_distribution.with_label_values(&[node_id]).set(count);\n    }\n\n    // ── Tasks ──\n\n    pub fn observe_task_processing_age(&self, age_secs: f64) {\n        self.task_processing_age.observe(age_secs);\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_tasks_total(&self, status: &str) {\n        self.tasks_total.with_label_values(&[status]).inc();\n    }\n\n    pub fn set_task_registry_size(&self, size: f64) {\n        self.task_registry_size.set(size);\n    }\n\n    // ── Rebalancer ──\n\n    pub fn set_rebalance_in_progress(&self, v: bool) {\n        self.rebalance_in_progress.set(if v { 1.0 } else { 0.0 });\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(&self, count: u64) {\n        self.rebalance_documents_migrated.inc_by(count as f64);\n    }\n\n    pub fn observe_rebalance_duration(&self, secs: f64) {\n        self.rebalance_duration.observe(secs);\n    }\n\n    // ── §13.11 Multi-search ──\n\n    pub fn observe_multisearch_queries_per_batch(&self, count: u64) {\n        if let Some(ref m) = self.multisearch_queries_per_batch {\n            m.observe(count as f64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_multisearch_batches_total(&self) {\n        if let Some(ref m) = self.multisearch_batches_total {\n            m.inc();\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_multisearch_partial_failures(&self) {\n        if let Some(ref m) = self.multisearch_partial_failures_total {\n            m.inc();\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override(&self, tenant: &str) {\n        if let Some(ref m) = self.multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total {\n            m.with_label_values(&[tenant]).inc();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── §13.12 Vector search ──\n\n    pub fn      if let Some(ref m) = self.vector_embedder_drift_total {\n            m.inc();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── §13.13 CDC ──\n\n    pub fn inc_cdc_events_published(&self, sink: &str, index: &str) test_request_id_generation() {\n        // Generate multiple IDs to verify format\n        for _ in 0..10 {\n            let id = generate_request_id();\n\n            // IDs should be 16 hex chars (64-bit hash)\n            assert_eq!(id.len(), 16);\n\n            // IDs should be hexadecimal\n            assert!(id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n        }\n\n        // Test that different UUID prefixes produce different IDs\n        let id1 = generate_request_id();\n        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(5));\n        let id2 = generate_request_id();\n        // In production, time ensures uniqueness; test just verifies format above\n        assert_eq!(id1.len(), 16);\n        assert_eq!(id2.len(), 16);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_metrics_creation() {\n        // Default config has all §13 features enabled\n        let metrics = Metrics::new(&MiroirConfig::default());\n\n        // Add some sample data to ensure metrics show up in output\n        metrics.request_duration.with_label_values(&[\"GET\", \"/test\", \"200\"]).observe(0.1);\n        metrics.requests_total.with_label_values(&[\"GET\", \"/test\", \"200\"]).inc();\n        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n        metrics.node_healthy.with_label_values(&[\"test-node\"]).set(1.0);\n        metrics.node_request_duration.with_label_values(&[\"test-node\", \"search\"]).observe(0.05);\n        metrics.node_errors.with_label_values(&[\"test-node\", \"timeout\"]).inc();\n        metrics.shard_coverage.set(1.0);\n        metrics.degraded_shards.set(0.0);\n        metrics.shard_distribution.with_label_values(&[\"test-node\"]).set(32.0);\n metrics.rebalance_documents_migrated.inc();\n        metrics.rebalance_duration.observe(10.0);\n\n        // Write to advanced Vec metrics so they appear in output\n metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(1.5);\n        metrics.set_request_queue_depth(42);\n        metrics.set_background_queue_depth(\"rebalance\", 5);\n        metrics.set_background_queue_depth(\"replication\", 3);\n        metrics.set_peer_pod_count(3);\n        metrics.set_leader(true);\n        metrics.set_owned_shards_count(12);\n\n        let encoded = metrics.encode_metrics();\n        assert!(encoded.is_ok());\n\n        let output = encoded.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify all 18 core plan §10 metric names appear in the output\n        let expected_metrics = [\n            // Request metrics\n            \"miroir_request_duration_seconds\",\n            \"miroir_requests_total\",\n            \"miroir_requests_in_flight\",\n            // Node health metrics\n            \"miroir_node_healthy\",\n            \"miroir_node_request_duration_seconds\",\n            \"miroir_node_errors_total\",\n            // Shard metrics\n            \"miroir_shard_coverage\",\n            \"miroir_degraded_shards_total\",\n            \"miroir_shard_distribution\",\n            // Task metrics\n            \"miroir_task_processing_age_seconds\",\n            \"miroir_tasks_total\",\n            \"miroir_task_registry_size\",\n            // Scatter-gather metrics\n            \"miroir_scatter_fan_out_size\",\n            \"miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total\",\n            \"miroir_scatter_retries_total\",\n            // Rebalancer metrics\n            \"miroir_rebalance_in_progress\",\n            \"miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total\",\n            \"miroir_rebalance_duration_seconds\",\n        ];\n        for name in &expected_metrics {\n            assert!(output.contains(name), \"missing metric: {}\", name);\n        }\n\n        // With defaults (all §13 enabled), advanced metrics should be present\n        let advanced_metrics = [\n            // §13.11 Multi-search\n            \"miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch\",\n            \"miroir_multisearch_batches_total\",\n #[test]\n    fn test_request_id_format() {\n        let id = RequestId::new();\n        // RequestId should be exactly 8 hex characters\n        assert_eq!(id.as_str().len(), 8);\n        assert!(id.as_str().chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_request_id_parse_valid() {\n        // Valid 8-char hex string\n        let valid = \"abcd1234\";\n        let parsed = RequestId::parse(valid.to_string());\n        assert!(parsed.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(parsed.unwrap().as_str(), valid);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_request_id_parse_invalid_wrong_length() {\n        // Wrong length (too short)\n        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"abc123\".to_string()).is_none());\n        // Wrong length (too long)\n        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"abcd12345678\".to_string()).is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_request_id_parse_invalid_non_hex() {\n        // Contains non-hex characters\n      // Generate two consecutive IDs - they should be different\n        // due to UUIDv7's timestamp component\n        let id1 = RequestId::new();\n        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(5));\n        let id2 = RequestId::new();\n\n        assert_ne!(id1, id2);\n        assert_ne!(id1.as_str(), id2.as_str());\n    }\n\n    // .uri(\"/test\")\n            .body(Full::default())\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Send the request\n        let response = app.oneshot(request).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify X-Request-Id header is present\n        let header = response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .expect(\"X-Request-Id header should be present\");\n        let #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_request_id_middleware_unique_per_request() {\n        use axum::{routing::get, Router};\n        use http_body_util::Full;\n        use tower::ServiceExt;\n\n        // Build a simple router with the request ID middleware\n        let app = Router::new()\n            .route(\"/test\", get(|| async { \"OK\" }))\n            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n        // Create two identical requests\n        let request1 = Request::builder()\n            .uri(\"/test\")\n            .body(Full::default())\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let request2 = Request::builder()\n            .uri(\"/test\")\n            .body(Full::default())\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Send both requests\n        let response1 = app.clone().oneshot(request1).await.unwrap();\n        let response2 = app.oneshot(request2).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Extract headers\n        let id1 = response1\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .unwrap()\n            .to_str()\n            .unwrap();\n        let id2 = response2\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .unwrap()\n            .to_str()\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Verify IDs are different (UUIDv7 timestamp ensures this)\n        assert_ne!(\n            id1, id2,\n            \"Two consecutive requests should have different request IDs\"\n        );\n\n        // Both should still be valid 8-char hex\n        assert_eq!(id1.len(), 8);\n P7.5 Structured JSON logging + request IDs + trace correlation tests.\n//!\n//! Tests for plan §10 structured logging format:\n//! - JSON-per-line parseable by jq\n//! - request_id on every log line (same as X-Request-Id header)\n//! - No PII (API keys, document content, query strings) in logs\n//! - Log volume: 2 INFO entries per search request (middleware + search handler)\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse axum::{\n    extract::Request,\n    body::Body,\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Router,\n};\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\n\n/// Helper: parse a JSON log line and extract fields\nfn parse_log_line(line: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {\n    serde_json::from_str(line).ok()\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a string is a valid 8-char hex request ID (from RequestId::new)\nfn is_valid_request_id(s: &str) -> bool {\n    s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit())\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a line contains PII patterns\nfn contains_pii(line: &str) -> bool {\n    let lower = line.to_lowercase();\n    // Check for API key patterns\n    if lower.contains(\"api_key\") || lower.contains(\"apikey\") || lower.contains(\"api-key\") {\n        // Allow if it's just \"api_key_hash\" or similar\n        if !lower.contains(\"hash\") && !lower.contains(\"uid\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    // Check for master keys\n    if lower.contains(\"master\") && (lower.contains(\"key\") || lower.contains(\"secret\")) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for JWT tokens\n    if lower.contains(\"eyj\") && lower.len() > 100 {\n        // JWT base64 pattern\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for document content patterns (long text blocks in logs)\n    if line.len() > 5000 {\n        // Very long log line might contain document content\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for raw query strings (both URL-style q= and JSON-style \"q\":)\n    if (lower.contains(\"q=\") || lower.contains(\"\\\"q\\\":\")) && line.len() > 200 {\n        // But not if the value is explicitly redacted\n        if !lower.contains(\"\\\"[redacted]\\\"\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    false\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 1: jq parses every log line\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_json_logs_parseable_by_jq() {\n    // Sample log entry in the expected format\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"GET /indexes/products/search 200\",\n        \"request_id\": \"abc123\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"miroir-proxy-7f8d9c6d5-abc12\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42,\n        \"status\": 200,\n        \"method\": \"GET\",\n        \"path_template\": \"/indexes/{uid}/search\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log);\n    assert!(parsed.is_some(), \"Sample log should parse as JSON\");\n\n    let log_obj = parsed.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"level\"], \"info\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"target\"], \"miroir.request\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"request_id\"], \"abc123\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"duration_ms\"], 42);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_format_in_logs() {\n    // Verify request_id appears in JSON logs\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"search completed\",\n        \"request_id\": \"a1b2c3d4\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"test-pod\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log).unwrap();\n    let request_id = parsed[\"request_id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n\n    // Request IDs should be 8 hex chars (from RequestId::new())\n    assert_eq!(request_id.len(), 8);\n    assert!(request_id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 2: request_id traceable across pods\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_extraction_from_logs() {\n    let logs = vec![\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"message\":\"GET /search 200\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.001Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"node_id\":\"node-1\",\"message\":\"node call started\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.010Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"index\":\"products\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    // Extract all logs with request_id = \"abc12345\"\n    let target_id = \"abc12345\";\n    let matching_logs: Vec<_> = logs\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(&format!(\"\\\"request_id\\\":\\\"{}\\\"\", target_id)))\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(matching_logs.len(), 3, \"Should find all 3 log lines for the request\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 3: No PII in logs\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_api_keys_in_logs() {\n    // These patterns should NOT appear in logs\n    let pii_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key\": \"master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"bearer\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"authorization\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in pii_patterns {\n        assert!(\n            contains_pii(pattern),\n            \"Pattern should be flagged as PII: {}\",\n            pattern\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Hashed versions are OK\n    let safe_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key_hash\": \"a1b2c3d4\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"key_uid\": \"12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"tenant_id\": \"tenant-123\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in test_no_query_strings_in_logs() {\n    // Raw Meilisearch query content should not be logged\n    // The contains_pii function checks for long q= patterns (user queries)\n    // Line must be >200 chars total and contain \"q=\" to trigger PII detection\n    let padding = \"x\".repeat(200);\n    let bad_log = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = 'test@example.com'\", \"padding\": \"{}\"}}\"#, padding);\n    assert!(contains_pii(&bad_log), \"Long query string should be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Redacted queries are OK\n    let good_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"query\": \"[redacted]\", \"q\": \"[redacted]\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(good_log), \"Redacted query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Short queries are OK (e.g., just a word or two)\n    let short_query = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"test\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(short_query), \"Short query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Long line without q= is OK\n    let long_safe = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing\", \"data\": \"{}\"}}\"#, \"x\".repeat(200));\n    assert!(!contains_pii(&long_safe), \"Long line without q= should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_document_content_in_logs() {\n    // Large JSON blocks likely contain document content\n    let large_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"indexing document\", \"doc\": {\"#;\n    let large_log_padded = format!(\"{}{}\", large_log, \"x\".repeat(5000));\n    assert!(contains_pii(&large_log_padded), \"Very long log line should be flagged as potential PII\");\n\n    // Normal log lines are OK\n    let normal_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"indexed 100 documents\", \"count\": 100}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(normal_log), \"Normal log line should NOT be flagged\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_log_volume_info_level() {\n    // At INFO level, search requests produce 2 INFO log entries:\n    // 1 r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.001Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"message\":\"node call\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.002Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    let info_count = request_logs\n        .iter()\n r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.003Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    let debug_count = debug_logs\n        .iter()\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_middleware_log_format() {\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{generate_request_id, RequestIdExt};\n test_log_levels_correct() {\n    let logs_by_level = vec![\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"error\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"internal failure\"}\"#, \"error\"),\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"warn\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"degraded response\"}\"#, \"warn\"),\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"GET / 200\"}\"#, \"info\"),\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"message\":\"node call\"}\"#, \"debug\"),\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"trace\",\"target\":\"miroir.scatter\",\"message\":\"fan-out buffer\"}\"#, \"trace\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (log, expected_level) in logs_by_level {\n        let parsed =
